title: Viery dal Gremio è solo il primo tassello della rivoluzione Fiorentina
excerpt: Colpo a sorpresa della Fiorentina che prende per 15 milioni di euro il difensore centrale Viery dal Gremio, terzo giocatore più costoso della storia del Brasilerao.
summary: Colpo a sorpresa della Fiorentina che prende per 15 milioni di euro il difensore centrale Viery dal Gremio, terzo giocatore più costoso della storia del Brasilerao. Perché questa decisione di Fabio Paratici? Cosa farà adesso? Sarà solo il primo tassello della rivoluzione gigliata. Ne parliamo nel Podcast di Tuttomercatoweb.com di oggi con Marco Conterio Atteso da lunedì a Firenze per visite e firma: Viery alla Fiorentina Nella giornata di ieri è andato il scena lo scambio di documenti fra le due società, con lo stesso Viery che è atteso a Firenze all'inizio della prossima settimana per sostenere le visite mediche di rito e mettere nero su bianco il suo nuovo contratto con la Fiorentina che avrà una durata quinquennale, quindi con scadenza a giugno del 2031.

title: Quote calciomercato Roma Brandt: Gasperini insiste per il tedesco
excerpt: Tra le opportunità che stanno animando il mercato della Roma c'è anche quella che porta a Julian Brandt.
summary: Tra le opportunità che stanno animando il mercato della Roma c'è anche quella che porta a Julian Brandt. Il fantasista tedesco, infatti, è destinato a lasciare il Borussia Dortmund nei prossimi giorni, essendo in scadenza di contratto. Un ruolo chiave nell'operazione potrebbe essere quello di Donyell Malen, suo ex compagno di squadra proprio in giallonero, che starebbe facendo da intermediario d'eccezione per favorire il possibile approdo del giocatore in Serie A. Intanto, nelle lavagne dei bookmakers è già possibile trovare le quote trasferimento Brandt alla Roma. title: Stasera in Panama-Inghilterra Kane può riscrivere la storia. Ma ricordate l'ultima volta?
excerpt: Le sfide delle 23 di questa sera determineranno le sorti del gruppo L.
summary: Le sfide delle 23 di questa sera determineranno le sorti del gruppo L. L'Inghilterra nonostante il pareggio contro il Ghana è ai sedicesimi di finale. Praticamente impossibile che la squadra di Tuchel non arrivi tra le prime due del raggruppamento: intanto a 90 minuti dal termine è prima, poi sfida una nazionale già eliminata e inoltre Croazia e Ghana nello scontro diretto si toglieranno punti a vicenda. Solo una sconfitta con tre gol di scarto degli inglesi contro Panama (e contemporanea vittoria di misura della Croazia) potrebbe far scivolare Kane e i suoi compagni al terzo posto. Utopia o quasi... Ma a quel punto l'Inghilterra avrebbe comunque l'ancora di salvataggio delle otto migliori terze. Se per gli inglesi, insomma, quella di stasera sarà quindi una gara da prove generali in vista della fase a eliminazione diretta, per Panama sarà l'occasione per mettersi in mostra per un'ultima volta dopo le sconfitte per 1-0 rimediate nelle prime due giornate. Stavolta alla nazionale del centro-america dovrebbe andare meglio rispetto al 2018 quando tornò a casa con undici gol incassati in tre gare, ma il rischio concreto è quello di tornarci esattamente come otto anni fa. Con zero punti. Ma ricordate contro chi Panama incassò la più pesante sconfitta in Russia? Proprio contro l'Inghilterra: la partita finì 6-1 con gol di Lingard, doppietta di Stones e tripletta di Kane. Una gara che per i Los Canaleros passò allo storia soprattutto perché fu quella in cui Panama realizzò il suo pri...

title: Malagò entra in azione: tanti nomi per la ricostruzione del Club Italia, c'è anche Boniek
excerpt: Si va verso dei cambiamenti profondi per dare nuovo slancio calcio italiano.
summary: Si va verso dei cambiamenti profondi per dare nuovo slancio calcio italiano. Giovanni Malagò, da poco eletto come nuovo presidente della Figc, sta valutando in questi giorni sul piano da impostare per ricostruire il Club Italia. Si partirà dalle risorse a disposizione, per poi impostare un piano di rilancio e soprattutto scegliere le persone giuste per portarlo avanti. Per quanto riguarda il discorso Nazionale, prima verrà nominato il direttore tecnico e poi il ct. L'obiettivo è nominare questa prima figura entro metà luglio e, per quanto riguarda le candidature, sono già stati fatti i nomi di vere e proprie eccellenze del nostro calcio: da Maldini a Baggio, fino ad arrivare a Del Piero e Buffon. Nomi nell'aria fin dall'eliminazione ai playoff contro la Bosnia, ai quali però ne va aggiunto un altro. Stando a quello che riferisce il Corriere dello Sport, tra coloro che potrebbero far parte del nuovo corso del Club Italia c'è anche Zbigniew Boniek. La sua esperienza come dirigente è superiore rispetto a quella dei 4 ex grandi giocatori azzurri, avendo guidato la Federazione polacca ed essendo stato anche stato vicepresidente Uefa. Un'idea di rottura con il passato: forse proprio quello che ci vuole.

title: Muslera, errore e sostituzione contro la Spagna: "Mai sofferto così tanto, perdonami Uruguay"
excerpt: Fernando Muslera ci ha messo la faccia dopo la dolorosa eliminazione dell'Uruguay dal Mondiale.
summary: Fernando Muslera ci ha messo la faccia dopo la dolorosa eliminazione dell'Uruguay dal Mondiale. Il portiere della Celeste, protagonista suo malgrado dell'errore che ha spalancato la strada al gol decisivo della Spagna, si è presentato in zona mista con grande amarezza, assumendosi tutte le responsabilità della serata. Parole sincere, quelle del veterano uruguaiano, che ha voluto chiedere scusa ai compagni e ai tifosi dopo una partita destinata a pesare a lungo nella sua carriera. "Non ho mai sofferto così tanto per questo sport e non avrei mai pensato di potermi sentire così male", ha confessato Muslera, ancora visibilmente scosso. Il quarantenne estremo difensore ha poi rivolto un messaggio diretto a tutto l'ambiente della Celeste: "Mi scuso con i miei compagni e con il popolo uruguaiano per il mio Mondiale. Anche se non sarà mai abbastanza". Una dichiarazione che fotografa il senso di colpa del portiere, sostituito all'intervallo dopo l'errore sul tiro di Alex Baena che ha deciso la sfida. Dopo la partita il ct Marcelo Bielsa aveva spiegato che il cambio era stato richiesto dallo stesso Muslera.

title: Ancelotti chases another record at 2026 World Cup
excerpt: One of the most successful coaches of all time, Carlo Ancelotti chases another record at...
summary: One of the most successful coaches of all time, Carlo Ancelotti chases another record at the 2026 World Cup. There’s just one coach in the world to have won the domestic title in each of the top five European leagues and the Champions League five times: ex-Milan boss Ancelotti. The 67-year-old has grabbed trophies at almost every club he’s coached, with just a few exceptions. Clubs like Chelsea, PSG, Real Madrid, Bayern Munich and Milan all lifted silverware under the Italian tactician in the last 20 years, but Carletto is eager to get even more.

title: How Cape Verde stunned World Cup to set up Argentina tie
excerpt: How has a group of islands with just 525,000 inhabitants made it to the knockout rounds of the World Cup?
summary: Cape Verde have reached the World Cup knockout stages on their debut appearance in the tournament Cape Verde have made history by becoming the smallest nation to reach the knockout stage at the World Cup. The prize for a side representing a group of 10 islands in the Atlantic Ocean will be to face defending champions Argentina in the last 32.

title: 'I leave nothing' - the end of 'toxic' Bielsa's Uruguay reign
excerpt: With Uruguay eliminated from the 2026 World Cup, BBC Sport looks at what has gone wrong for them under colourful coach Marcelo Bielsa.
summary: Muslera mistake gifts Spain win as 10-man Uruguay crash out 'in disgrace' In substituting a national team hero at half-time before delivering a brutally honest self-reflection, the end of Marcelo Bielsa's tenure as Uruguay manager felt rather fitting. A 1-0 defeat by Spain in Guadalajara condemned the two-time World Cup winners to a humiliating second successive group-stage exit, and neatly summed up Bielsa's struggles as head coach.

title: England qualify for World Cup knockout stage
excerpt: England's place in the knockout stages of the World Cup is confirmed by other results before they face Panama in their final group game on Saturday.
summary: England won their World Cup opener against Croatia but drew with Ghana England's place in the knockout stages of the World Cup has been confirmed before they face Panama in their final group game on Saturday. The Three Lions sit on four points after two games - meaning they are at least guaranteed one of the eight third-placed qualification spots.

summary: The rules are simple. Each day there's a new footballer and the challenge is to guess who they are in as few attempts as possible. After each wrong guess you unlock a new clue. summary: Shoja Khalilzadeh's goal is ruled out by VAR in added time to deny Iran automatic qualification for the last 32 - and they must now hope to go through as one of the best third-placed sides.

title: ‘This must never happen again’: Iran coach hits out at the US and tells Infantino to stand up against hosts
excerpt:
• Iran waiting to see if they are through to last 32

  • Salah injury fears played down after 1-1 draw

Iran’s head coach, Amir Ghalenoei, has said Fifa’s president, Gianni Infantino, must “stand up” to the US after reiterating his belief that the co-hosts have tre
summary: Iran’s head coach, Amir Ghalenoei, has said Fifa’s president, Gianni Infantino, must “stand up” to the US after reiterating his belief that the co-hosts have treated his team “very unfairly”. Iran will qualify for the World Cup knockout stage for the first time if results go their way in the next 24 hours, but after a dramatic draw against Egypt in Seattle, in which Shoja Khalilzadeh had a stoppage-time winner ruled offside and Saeid Ezatolah headed against the crossbar, they were left frustrated with more than just the result. Infantino visited Iran’s dressing room after their Group G opener against New Zealand, telling the squad “you are stronger than everything”, but while Ghalenoei maintains Infantino has tried his utmost to help them, ultimately little has changed since the tournament began. The head coach previously described Iran as the “most oppressed” team at the World Cup.

title: Uruguay slump to shock early World Cup exit after Baena strike sends Spain through
excerpt: Uruguay are out of the World Cup again and for all that it ended in anger, a red card and a touchline row, it is nobody’s fault but their own. “3 million dreams,” the banner said high in the stand in Guadalajara but this was a recurring nightmare. Divided and dysfunctional, led by a man who barel
summary: Uruguay are out of the World Cup again and for all that it ended in anger, a red card and a touchline row, it is nobody’s fault but their own. “3 million dreams,” the banner said high in the stand in Guadalajara but this was a recurring nightmare. Divided and dysfunctional, led by a man who barely even says buenos dias to his players, unable to get beyond a draw with Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde, on the night when it was all or nothing against Spain they could only manage two shots on target, neither of which came before the 80th minute or carried even the slightest threat. Spain meanwhile had only one, and that didn’t carry much of a threat either. But a dreadful mistake from 40-year-old goalkeeper Fernando Muslera allowed Álex Baena’s shot to slip into the net just before the half-time break. Uruguay fought but didn’t play much football and so they fell. For the second World Cup running they failed to get through the group and if four years ago that was on goals scored and could be explained by the teams they faced – South Korea, Ghana and Portugal – this time the opponents only made it worse, demanding a far deeper analysis. “It’s my responsibility; we had a potential that I didn’t manage to turn into a team that played to its level,” Marcelo Bielsa said. Muslera had asked to be withdrawn at half-time, his fourth World Cup ending early, but the guilt was all of theirs. On the hour Federico Valverde too was withdrawn, this time against his will, and departed with his shirt p...

title: Cape Verde continue fairytale World Cup debut after Saudi Arabia draw books last-32 spot
excerpt: After the full-time whistle Cape Verde’s players formed a huddle around their head coach, Bubista, eyes straining at the tiny moving images on a mobile phone. They sought the certainty that a dream had come true and, when the outcome nearly 1,000 miles away in Guadalajara was confirmed, erupted i
summary: After the full-time whistle Cape Verde’s players formed a huddle around their head coach, Bubista, eyes straining at the tiny moving images on a mobile phone. They sought the certainty that a dream had come true and, when the outcome nearly 1,000 miles away in Guadalajara was confirmed, erupted in unfathomable joy. Dailon Livramento, the centre-forward, leapt on to the back of his teammate “Diney” Borges. Everyone in view grabbed the nearest person to embrace and then came all the flags, the islands represented by their 10 stars made famous during one of the World Cup’s most compelling underdog stories in decades. One of them was waved in the stands by Ana Cândida Évora, the mother of their remarkable goalkeeper Vozinha. Others made their way on to the pitch and what a sight it was when the entire squad, visibly buzzing to a man, stayed still for long enough to pose for photographs in front of a disbelieving support. They drummed and sang into the night because never has the most formidable of tasks seemed so glorious. Cape Verde, the country of 530,000, will take on Lionel Messi and Argentina in the last 32. Respect will need paying to a veteran of barely imaginable global fame. Yes, Argentina will have to fully acknowledge the prowess of Vozinha, the 40-year-old who has become a sensation in real life and online since the display that thwarted Spain. The meeting between Vozinha, who was playing in the São Vicente island league at the age of 29, and the tournament’s highest ...

title: Five-star Belgium through as New Zealand sent packing from World Cup
excerpt:
Leandro Trossard struck twice as Belgium demolished New Zealand 5-1 to clinch top spot in Group G and march into the World Cup’s last 32, ⁠while the All Whites’ tournament dreams ⁠crumbled.

Belgium finished above Egypt on ​goal difference after they both ended with five points from three ma
summary: Leandro Trossard struck twice as Belgium demolished New Zealand 5-1 to clinch top spot in Group G and march into the World Cup’s last 32, ⁠while the All Whites’ tournament dreams ⁠crumbled. Belgium finished above Egypt on ​goal difference after they both ended with five points from three matches, followed by Iran on three points and New Zealand on one. In the other match in the group, Egypt drew 1-1 with Iran. Belgium ⁠will next face one of the eight best third-placed teams. “We have performed very well,” Trossard said. “We are happy now because we’re number one. Now we have to go to the round of 32, and we’re all ready to deliver.

title: Why England's backline remains Tuchel's biggest conundrum
excerpt: England were stifled creatively against Ghana as the Three Lions' second-game curse struck again in perhaps its most frustrating iteration - but do Thomas Tuchel's biggest worries lie at the other end of the pitch?
summary: England have given up just 0.87xG against Croatia and Ghana but looked defensively exposed at different times in both games; five of seven World Cup winners since expansion in 1998 have kept five clean sheets at World Cup finals; Reece James potentially out for tournament with injury England were stifled creatively against Ghana as the Three Lions' second-game curse struck again in perhaps its most frustrating iteration - but do Thomas Tuchel's biggest worries lie at the other end of the pitch? England have given away just 0.87xG across their opening two games and faced only a solitary shot on target against Ghana on Tuesday night.

title: Yoon widens Women's PGA Championship lead to five strokes with Korda in chase
excerpt: Ina Yoon stretched her lead at the Women's PGA Championship with a three-under 69 that put her five strokes up on second-place pack after another strong showing from the South Korean.
summary: Second day of Women's PGA Championship sees Ina Yoon stretch lead to five; Nelly Korda six shots off lead as English duo Charley Hull, Lottie Woad drop from contention; Watch KPMG Women's PGA Championship third round coverage live on Saturday from 5pm on Sky Sports Golf Ina Yoon stretched her lead at the Women's PGA Championship with a three-under 69 that put her five strokes up on second-place pack after another strong showing from the South Korean. Yoon, who shot a record 63 in the first round at Hazeltine National Golf Club, had two bogeys with one birdie on the back nine to nudge to 12 under. The 23-year-old is seeking her first LPGA Tour victory.

title: Humphries claims back-to-back US Darts Masters titles after beating Littler
excerpt: Luke Humphries retained the US Darts Masters title with a thrilling deciding-leg victory against Luke Littler on a record-breaking night in New York.
summary: Day two of US Darts Masters at Madison Square Gardens sees Luke Humphries sensationally defend his title; Humphries survived match darts to beat Gian van Veen, and then beat Luke Littler 8-7 in final; watch World Matchplay live on Sky Sports in Blackpool July 18-26 Luke Humphries retained the US Darts Masters title with a thrilling deciding-leg victory against Luke Littler on a record-breaking night in New York. Humphries created another slice of history at Madison Square Garden, becoming the first player to win back-to-back titles in the Big Apple.

title: Iran inches away from famous win but still have World Cup hope after Egypt draw
excerpt: Iran had a last-gasp goal overturned as they just missed out on grabbing second spot in Group G - but a first-ever progression to the World Cup knockouts could still be achieved after their dramatic 1-1 draw with Egypt.
summary: Match report as Iran have a late goal overturned and hit the bar in stoppage time to finish third in Group G; Iran can still progress as one of best third-place sides; Egypt drop to second with dramatic draw in Seattle and face Australia in last-32 Iran had a last-gasp goal overturned as they just missed out on grabbing second spot in Group G - but a first-ever progression to the World Cup knockouts could still be achieved after their dramatic 1-1 draw with Egypt. Shoja Khalilzadeh's foot was spotted just ahead of play on the offside review after he had pounced on a rebound in injury-time to spark wild celebrations in Seattle.

title: Italia reactiva su interés por Juanlu
excerpt: Atalanta y Fiorentina han sido los dos últimos clubes en preguntar por la situación del jugador
summary: Atalanta y Fiorentina han sido los dos últimos clubes en preguntar por la situación del jugador Fiorentina y Atalanta han realizado un primer sondeo para conocer las pretensiones del Sevilla y las condiciones por un traspaso de Juanlu Sánchez, según informó Matteo Moretto. La entidad nervionense necesita hacer caja y el canterano puede ser una de las grandes ventas del verano. Italia vuelve a la carga por el jugador de Montequinto. El Nápoles intentó hacerse con Juanlu el pasado verano. Giovanni Manna, director deportivo del club partenopeo, estuvo en la capital hispalense hace un año para sentarse con los directivos del Sevilla y en invierno hizo un nuevo intento, ofreciendo la opción de un intercambio o incluso una cesión con opción de compra. Pero no cuajó.

title: Ya no habrá que trasnochar más con España, que se gana el prime time: fechas y horarios del camino a la final
excerpt: La selección, primera de grupo, jugará en el horario estrella todas las eliminatorias del Mundial si sigue avanzando: dieciseisavos, octavos, cuartos, semifinales y final serían siempre a las nueve de la noche
summary: La selección, primera de grupo, jugará en el horario estrella todas las eliminatorias del Mundial si sigue avanzando: dieciseisavos, octavos, cuartos, semifinales y final serían siempre a las nueve de la noche España, con su éxito antes Uruguay (0-1), no sólo se ha ganado el billete para las eliminatorias del Mundial. También se ha ganado el escaparate. La selección española, después de cerrar la fase de grupos como primera, afrontará el tramo decisivo del torneo con una ventaja añadida de calendario: todos sus posibles partidos hasta la final están fijados en horario de prime time. Un recorrido perfecto desde el punto de vista competitivo, televisivo y emocional. España evita horarios incómodos y se instala en la franja de máxima audiencia, justo cuando el Mundial entra en su fase más caliente. La condición de primera de grupo le permite a la selección ordenar su camino con fechas claras, descanso medido entre partidos y una exposición total ante la afición.

title: Canobbio se defiende tras sus entradas a Pedri y Cubarsí: "No fui con la plancha"
excerpt: El delantero del Fluminense, expulsado en la derrota de Uruguay, negó haber ido con la plancha en la acción sobre el central azulgrana
summary: El delantero del Fluminense, expulsado en la derrota de Uruguay, negó haber ido con la plancha en la acción sobre el central azulgrana Agustín Canobbio terminó el partido ante España en el centro de la escena. Uruguay cayó eliminada del Mundial 2026 a manos de la selección española (0-1) y el delantero del Fluminense, de 27 años, acabó expulsado después de dos entradas durísimas: una a Pedri y otra a Pau Cubarsí, la más salvaje de las dos. Al final del encuentro, el uruguayo fue entrevistado para hablar de la eliminación y para dar su versión de esas dos acciones. Canobbio también comparó el partido ante España con los anteriores encuentros de Uruguay frente a Arabia Saudí y Cabo Verde. A su juicio, el equipo había estado por encima en esos dos duelos, aunque no logró cerrar los detalles que después terminaron pesando. "¿Si ha sido nuestro mejor partido del Mundial? En los otros dos partidos fuimos superiores, pero no supimos minimizar los detalles que tuvimos que minimizar y ellos fueron más efectivos a la hora de llegar", dijo.

title: "Ha sido un partido muy violento, el árbitro ha estado lamentable"
summary: Miguel Ángel Lara y José Luis Allegue analizan la victoria de España ante Uruguay, la clasificación para la siguiente ronda y las lesiones de Nico y Yeremy que dejan a la selección en jaque.

title: Nicolas Pépé encuentra la paz: "Fue una de las mejores noches de mi carrera"
excerpt: El atacante del Villarreal lideró la histórica clasificación de Costa de Marfil a las eliminatorias del Mundial con un doblete a Curazao tras quedarse fuera de la Copa África por cuestiones extradeportivas
summary: El atacante del Villarreal lideró la histórica clasificación de Costa de Marfil a las eliminatorias del Mundial con un doblete a Curazao tras quedarse fuera de la Copa África por cuestiones extradeportivas Nacido en Francia, siempre hizo caso a sus raíces y, en 2016, debutó con Costa de Marfil a los 21 años. "Quería jugar para el país de mis padres", explicaba por aquel entonces un Nicolas Pépé que acababa de regresar al Angers de su cesión en el Orleans. Sólo un año después, fue traspasado al Lille por 18 millones. En ese momento, la venta más cara en la historia del club. Fueron dos temporadas de ensueño las que protagonizó por la banda derecha del Pierre-Mauroy. Ya su primer año, saldado con 14 goles y cuatro asistencias, fue notable. Pero los 23+12 que firmó al curso siguiente le valieron para entrar en el once ideal de la Ligue 1... antes de que el Arsenal 'saltase la banca' para entregarle su fichaje a Unai Emery.

title: Genoa, accordo verbale col Thun per Meichtry: De Rossi attende il suo nuovo centrocampista
excerpt: Il Genoa è vicino a chiudere un acquisto in mediana.
summary: Il Genoa è vicino a chiudere un acquisto in mediana. Come riporta Sky Sport, il club rossoblù ha raggiunto infatti un accordo verbale con il Thun per l'arrivo di Franz-Ethan Meichtry, centrocampista svizzero classe 2005. Restano da definire solo gli ultimi dettagli formali prima della chiusura dell'operazione. Per il giovane talento, cresciuto e affermatosi in patria con la maglia del Thun, si profila così il primo trasferimento all'estero. Il Genoa avrebbe superato la concorrenza di alcune neopromosse della Premier League, assicurandosi uno dei profili più interessanti del calcio svizzero. Una volta completato l'iter burocratico, Meichtry sarà a disposizione della squadra guidata da Daniele De Rossi.

title: Berti e la Nazionale: "Dieci giorni indimenticabili. Vestire l'Azzurro è il sogno più grande"
excerpt: Nel corso dell'intervista rilasciata ai canali ufficiali della Cremonese dopo il suo arrivo dal Cesena, il centrocampista Tommaso Berti ha parlato anche della sua recente esperienza con la Nazionale maggiore nelle amichevoli contro Lussemburgo e Grecia...
summary: Nel corso dell'intervista rilasciata ai canali ufficiali della Cremonese dopo il suo arrivo dal Cesena, il centrocampista Tommaso Berti ha parlato anche della sua recente esperienza con la Nazionale maggiore nelle amichevoli contro Lussemburgo e Grecia in cui sulla panchina si è seduto il tecnico dell'Under 21 Silvio Baldini. Queste le sue parole: Lo scorso giugno hai ricevuto la chiamata della Nazionale maggiore. Cosa si prova in quei momenti? “Le prime persone che ho chiamato sono stati i miei genitori. Ho vissuto dieci giorni indimenticabili con emozioni grandissime e incredibili, perché penso che giocare per l’Italia sia il sogno più grande di qualsiasi giocatore italiano. In più abbiamo vinto due partite e ho avuto la fortuna di allenarmi con campioni come Donnarumma e Pio Esposito, oltre a tanti compagni fortissimi”. Con l’Italia Under 21 hai avuto modo di allenarti al Centro Sportivo “Giovanni Arvedi” e vivere una partita allo Zini. Che esperienza è stata? “Il centro mi ha fatto veramente una bella impressione e ci ha portato bene, perché abbiamo vinto contro l’Armenia. Non vedo l’ora di tornare ad allenarmi lì”.

title: Mondiale 2026, classifica marcatori aggiornata: Messi in vetta, quattro big alle sue spalle
excerpt: Continuano ad arrivare gol a pioggia al Mondiale 2026.
summary: 4 gol: Kylian Mbappe (Francia), Erling Haaland (Norvegia), Vinicius Jr (Brasile), Ousmane Dembélé (Francia). 3 gol: Deniz Undav (Germania), Jonathan David (Canada), Johan Manzambi (Svizzera), Ismael Saibari (Marocco), Matheus Cunha (Brasile), Brian Brobbey (Olanda), Ismaila Sarr (Senegal). 2 gol: Mikel Oyarzabal (Spagna), Yasin Ayari (Svezia), Anthony Elanga (Svezia), Cody Gakpo (Olanda), Crysencio Summerville (Olanda), Folarin Balogun (Stati Uniti), Cyle Larin (Canada), Maximiliano Araujo (Uruguay), Daichi Kamada (Giappone), Ayase Ueda (Giappone), Elijah Just (Nuova Zelanda), Harry Kane (Inghilterra), Kai Havertz (Germania), Daniel Munoz (Colombia), Cristiano Ronaldo (Portogallo), Ruben Vargas (Svizzera), Ermin Mahmic (Bosnia ed Erzegovina), Julian Quinones (Messico), Nicolas Pepe (Costa d'Avorio), Pape Gueye (Senegal).

title: Ora l'Inter infastidisce il Napoli: inserimento nella trattativa per Khalaili sulla destra
excerpt: Sfumato Marco Palestra, l’Inter valuta alternative sulla fascia destra e potrebbe dare fastidio al Napoli.
summary: Sfumato Marco Palestra, l’Inter valuta alternative sulla fascia destra e potrebbe dare fastidio al Napoli. Secondo quanto riferito da Sky Sport, il club nerazzurro sarebbe infatti pronto a muoversi per Anan Khalaili, laterale dell’Union Saint-Gilloise da tempo nel mirino del club partenopeo. Il Napoli, per ora, non ha ancora raggiunto un accordo con il club belga, che valuta l’esterno israeliano classe 2004 attorno ai 25 milioni di euro. Da capire se l’arrivo dell’Inter possa cambiare le carte in tavola. I nerazzurri hanno necessità di un colpo in quel settore del campo, visto il trasferimento - non ancora ufficiale - di Denzel Dumfries al Real Madrid. Le alternative restano varie, da Cambiaso a Dodo, passando per Ndoye e Norton-Cuffy.

title: Claudio Terzi lascia la Primavera dello Spezia e va in Serie D: allenerà il Ligorna
excerpt: L'esperienza di Claudio Terzi alla guida della Primavera dello Spezia, club  in cui ha militato e di cui è stato capitano, è terminata con l'allenatore che dalla prossima stagione sarà alla guida di una prima squadra in Serie D.
summary: L'esperienza di Claudio Terzi alla guida della Primavera dello Spezia, club in cui ha militato e di cui è stato capitano, è terminata con l'allenatore che dalla prossima stagione sarà alla guida di una prima squadra in Serie D. L'ex del Bologna infatti si siederà sulla panchina del Ligorna come comunicato dalla società ligure in una nota ufficiale: "La Società SCD Ligorna 1922 è lieta di comunicare di aver affidato la guida tecnica della Prima Squadra a Claudio Terzi nella stagione 2026/27 . ⠀ Classe 1984, Terzi approda al Ligorna dopo l'esperienza maturata nel Settore Giovanile dello Spezia, dove nelle ultime due stagioni ha guidato prima l'Under 17 e successivamente la formazione Primavera, nelle ultime due stagioni. In precedenza aveva fatto parte dello staff tecnico della prima squadra bianconera, vivendo da vicino il calcio professionistico anche nella nuova veste di allenatore. ⠀ Da calciatore, Claudio Terzi vanta una carriera di altissimo profilo con oltre vent'anni di attività tra Serie A, Serie B e Serie C, avendo collezionato più di 550 presenze. Cresciuto nel Bologna, ha vestito anche le maglie di Napoli, Siena, Palermo e soprattutto Spezia, club di cui è stato capitano e simbolo, conquistando la storica promozione in Serie A del 2020. ⠀ A Mister Terzi il più caloroso benvenuto da parte di tutta la famiglia biancazzurra e i migliori auguri di buon lavoro per questa nuova avventura sulla panchina del Ligorna".

title: Lazio fans protest and Lotito completes purchase of new club
excerpt: Lazio fans have announced a mass protest on July 2 and they were further angered...
summary: Lazio fans have announced a mass protest on July 2 and they were further angered when it was announced President Claudio Lotito had bought another club, Reggina. The ultras have been boycotting the home matches for months at the Stadio Olimpico, leaving the stands painfully deserted during even the biggest games of the season in Serie A. They only suspended their protest for a couple of exceptions, most notably the Coppa Italia Final defeat to Inter.

title: Norton-Cuffy attractive to Juventus, Napoli and Fiorentina
excerpt: Genoa full-back Brooke Norton-Cuffy is set to spark a bidding war, with reports Juventus, Napoli...
summary: Genoa full-back Brooke Norton-Cuffy is set to spark a bidding war, with reports Juventus, Napoli and Fiorentina have all asked about the ex-Arsenal talent. The 22-year-old had a very impressive two years at Marassi since the transfer from the Gunners for €2m plus add-ons and a percentage of the sell-on fee. A product of the Arsenal academy, his value has soared thanks to regular performances for the Grifone, both as a right-back and in a more advanced role in a five-man midfield.

title: Jorge Mendes mastermind of Milan transfers, after Goncalo Ramos, targeted Casadó
excerpt: Jorge Mendes is emerging as the key behind the new Milan transfer strategy, as after...
summary: Jorge Mendes is emerging as the key behind the new Milan transfer strategy, as after a €70m deal for PSG striker Goncalo Ramos, he’s working to bring in Marc Casadó from Barcelona. It is hardly new that agent Mendes has very strong links with the Rossoneri, as he already helped engineer moves for Pervis Estupinan, Joao Felix, Andre Silva and of course coach Sergio Conceicao, amongst others. Now that Milan have scrapped the roles of technical director and director of sport for a more streamlined club structure, coach Ruben Amorim is given more power, and that also means consulting with Mendes.

title: Legendary Vieira: ‘Koné is the best midfielder in France today’
excerpt: Arsenal legend and former Genoa coach Patrick Vieira declared that Roma’s Manu Koné is ‘for...
summary: Arsenal legend and former Genoa coach Patrick Vieira declared that Roma’s Manu Koné is ‘for me today the best midfielder in France.’ Les Bleus complete the group stage of the World Cup this evening against Norway at 20.00 UK time (21.00 CEST). He has 16 senior caps for France, but was left on the bench for the opening World Cup game against Senegal, then started the next two with Iraq and Norway.

title: Report Davinson Sanchez and Galatasaray in talks for Como move
excerpt: Como are in negotiations with Galatasaray for Colombia international Davinson Sanchez, claim reports, with an...
summary: Como are in negotiations with Galatasaray for Colombia international Davinson Sanchez, claim reports, with an asking price of €15m for the former Tottenham defender. According to Belgian transfer expert Sacha Tavolieri, the talks are in full swing and the player is open to a Serie A switch to work with coach Cesc Fabregas. It’s the second year in a row that Como have been linked with a move for the player.

title: Brilliant Dembele hat-trick helps France see off Norway to top group
excerpt: Ousmane Dembele's first-half hat-trick helps France soundly defeat a Norway side without Erling Haaland 4-1 to top Group I at the World Cup.
summary: Ousmane Dembele's first-half hat-trick helps France soundly defeat a Norway side without Erling Haaland 4-1 to top Group I at the World Cup.

title: What each team need to reach World Cup knockouts
excerpt: With the final round of group fixtures nearing a conclusion the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.
summary: Spain will progress if they avoid defeat against Uruguay in their final group game The final round of group fixtures is nearing a conclusion, with the path to the World Cup final on 19 July becoming clearer. But with 32 of the 48 teams advancing to the knockout rounds, new tie-breaker rules, and a table of third-placed teams to keep track of, staying across the various permutations is far from simple.

title: Dembélé hat-trick fires France past Norway to seal top spot in World Cup group
excerpt: Does anyone at the World Cup have the defensive strength to deny this hyper-mobile, supremely varied French attack? Not, it turns out, a second-string Norway, who were torn apart in the first half in Boston, as Ousmane Dembélé scored a beautifully precise 25-minute hat-trick en route to a 4-1 win
summary: Does anyone at the World Cup have the defensive strength to deny this hyper-mobile, supremely varied French attack? Not, it turns out, a second-string Norway, who were torn apart in the first half in Boston, as Ousmane Dembélé scored a beautifully precise 25-minute hat-trick en route to a 4-1 win. France now top Group I and will play their last-32 tie in New Jersey next Tuesday. Norway will play Côte d’Ivoire in Texas. This was a fun, freewheeling game, with the feel of a tournament formality, big third-place playoff energy. But it wasn’t quite that. Certainly nothing that happened here in the absence of the rested Erling Haaland will help nourish Norway’s late-stage tournament hopes, which are real but tentative, and which can only have been damaged by the sight of France’s attack using the corners of Egil Selvik’s goal for shooting practice in a jarringly open, defensively chaotic first half. This game had been billed in the World Cup hype-o-drome as Mbappé v Haaland, Nordic Goal-Grendel versus Parisian Attack-Guillotine, and so on. In the event, it was something else as Haaland was rested along with nine other Norwegians, plus any notions of continuity, momentum or coherent defence.

title: Senegal salvage hopes of a place in World Cup last 32 with thrashing of Iraq
excerpt:
Substitute Pape Gueye scored a pair of sensational second-half goals from long range, and Senegal got a much-needed boost to their goal differencewith a 5-0 victory over 10-man Iraq in their Group I finale on Friday.

Ismaïla Sarr added his third ⁠goal of the tournament, ⁠the first ​of four
summary: Substitute Pape Gueye scored a pair of sensational second-half goals from long range, and Senegal got a much-needed boost to their goal difference with a 5-0 victory over 10-man Iraq in their Group I finale on Friday. Ismaïla Sarr added his third ⁠goal of the tournament, ⁠the first ​of four after half-time for the Lions of Teranga, who sealed a third-place group finish and are vying for one ⁠of the eight third-place spots in the last 32. They currently hold the fifth-best record of third-place sides based on their plus-2 goal difference, but ⁠four of the teams beneath them have yet to play their final group matches.

title: England’s Reece James ruled out for at least two games with hamstring injury
excerpt:
• Tuchel now left with limited regular full-back options

  • Chelsea defender sustained hamstring setback in March

Reece James will miss England’s next two World Cup matches at least as he fights to recover from a hamstring injury. The right-back, who has a histor
summary: Tuchel now left with limited regular full-back options Chelsea defender sustained hamstring setback in March Reece James will miss England’s next two World Cup matches at least as he fights to recover from a hamstring injury. The right-back, who has a history of hamstring problems, reported tightness after the team’s 0-0 draw against Ghana in Boston on Tuesday.

title: US Soccer offers contract renewal to Mauricio Pochettino through 2030 World Cup
excerpt:
• Pochettino has been in charge of the US since late 2024

  • Former Spurs and PSG boss likely to have club interest

Mauricio Pochettino has been offered a contract extension that would keep him in charge of the US men’s national team through the 2030 World Cup, m
summary: Pochettino has been in charge of the US since late 2024 Former Spurs and PSG boss likely to have club interest Mauricio Pochettino has been offered a contract extension that would keep him in charge of the US men’s national team through the 2030 World Cup, multiple sources familiar with the offer said on Friday. Sources spoke with the Guardian on condition of anonymity because they were not authorized to speak on the matter.

title: Algeria eye revenge in first match against Austria since ‘shame of Gijón’
excerpt:
Group J game recalls the 1982 World Cup, when Austria and West Germany played out a mutually beneficial result

How long do football grudges last? It has long been argued that Brazil never truly recovered from
summary: Group J game recalls the 1982 World Cup, when Austria and West Germany played out a mutually beneficial result How long do football grudges last? It has long been argued that Brazil never truly recovered from the Maracanazo, or, if they did, it took decades. The same might be said of the English football community and Diego’ Maradona’s “Hand of God”. In Algeria, the national wound was inflicted at the 1982 World Cup and has been universally referred to as “the Shame of Gijón”. Almost exactly 44 years later, a potential reckoning for that arrives when Algeria play Austria in their final group match, with a last-32 place on the line. In the summer of 1982 Algeria was a young nation, two decades removed from 132 years of colonial rule. Unless one was versed in African independence movements or the doctrine of third-worldism, Algeria was unlikely to register in most people’s political thinking. The World Cup, held in Spain, was a rare and precious platform for the country to announce itself, and the team seized the opportunity.

title: Connor shines as leaders Leeds produce statement victory against Hull KR
excerpt: Leeds strengthened their position at the Betfred Super League summit after delivering another crushing win over defending champions Hull KR at Headingley.
summary: Leeds strengthened their position at the Betfred Super League summit after delivering another crushing win over defending champions Hull KR at Headingley. Eight days after slaying fellow title rivals Warrington by a similar margin, Brad Arthur's men turned on the style to sink the Robins 34-8 as Jake Connor helped himself to an 18-point individual haul. Coupled with another brace from Maika Sivo, it added up to an emphatic statement of intent from the hosts who doled out more misery on their opponents after their 58-6 demolition during their last meeting in Las Vegas in February.

title: Scheffler misses out on another sub-60 round for early Travelers target
excerpt: Scottie Scheffler failed to convert a 25-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole at the Travelers Championship, narrowly missing out on a chance to join Jim Furyk as the only players in PGA Tour history with two sub-60 rounds.
summary: Scottie Scheffler sets an early target atop the scoreboard at Travelers Championship as he misses out on a second sub-60 round; "It would be cool to shoot 59, but somebody has already shot 58 here, so it's not even the course record" Scottie Scheffler failed to convert a 25-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole at the Travelers Championship, narrowly missing out on a chance to join Jim Furyk as the only players in PGA Tour history with two sub-60 rounds. Scheffler had to settle for a 10-under 60 on the soft TPC River Highlands course, setting an early target atop the scoreboard on a day of extreme low scoring at the final signature event of the regular season.

title: Golden Boot race: Dembele joins Mbappe in Messi chase after hat-trick
excerpt: Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappe, Erling Haaland, Vinicius Junior and now Ousmane Dembele are involved in what is shaping up to be one of the most hotly contested World Cup Golden Boot battles in memory.
summary: Ousmane Dembele joins goalscoring pack chasing Lionel Messi in race for World Cup Golden Boot after hat-trick vs Norway; Cristiano Ronaldo finally off mark, Harry Kane in mix; Just Fontaine's 1958 record of 13 goals in a single tournament is in sight Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappe, Erling Haaland, Vinicius Junior and now Ousmane Dembele are involved in what is shaping up to be one of the most hotly contested World Cup Golden Boot battles in memory. The superstars are delivering on the biggest stage of them all and are setting a blistering pace in the race to be the 2026 tournament's top goalscorer.
